data.body:
G4 (child consistency) container review — parent epic `5fd2` × child `b711` (Triggered overlays). Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster; high-confidence + benign-reading filter applied. ONE finding (mode: CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS + RESIDUAL REFERENCES, with cross-sibling CONSUMER IMPACT). Clean modes affirmed at the end.

═══ FINDING (modes: CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS + RESIDUAL REFERENCES + CONSUMER IMPACT) — b711's shipped T12 routing (DETERMINISTIC keyword) CONTRADICTS the parent's settled FINALIZE CORRECTION (T12 is LLM-ROUTED for plans), b711's OWN Scope, and the canonical descriptor source ═══
- criteria: [G4]
- location: b711 deliverable `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:275-280` (`_DET_OVERLAY_RULES`) lists `"T12": r"\b(deploy|rollout|canary|feature flag|production traffic|rollback|blue.green)\b"` — i.e. T12 fires DETERMINISTICALLY on a keyword. Contradicted by:
  (a) b711's OWN `## Scope`: "T10/T11/T12 are LLM-routed for plans (E4-confirmed: deterministic keyword over-fires on rebar's bash->py 'migration' / ubiquitous 'deploy')."
  (b) Parent `5fd2` body, "Overlay triggering" + the FINALIZE CORRECTION: "the new resource overlays T10/T11/T12 are LLM-ROUTED for PLAN review, NOT deterministic — ... det T12 'deploy'/'rollback' fired 7/19, all false; the LLM router fired 0."
  (c) Canonical descriptor source `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/make_crit_v8.py:29`: `LLM_ROUTED = {"T10","T11","T12","T8","T6","G6","T9"}` — T12 is in the LLM-routed set, NOT the deterministic set.
- evidence:
  - The shipped deterministic rule (`registry.py:279`) keys T12 on `deploy|rollout|...|rollback` — precisely the keyword the parent's round-4 experiment found over-fires (7/19, all false) on rebar's own prose, which is why the design CORRECTED T12 to LLM-routed. The implementation re-introduces the exact false-fire mode the parent settled against.
  - This is NOT a one-off transcription slip: b711's own verify comments DISAGREE with each other on T12's routing. The first verify-completion note (comment 1) and the second (comment 3) both list the deterministic low-FP set as "T5a/T5d/T7/T12" / "T5a, T5d, T7, T12-keyword" (T12 = DETERMINISTIC), while the same comments' "AC 2" sections cite `criteria_routing.json:1135` as `overlay_routing: "llm"` for T12 (T12 = LLM-routed). So the descriptor JSON and the runtime `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` disagree, and b711 verified itself PASS without reconciling them.
  - This was ALREADY independently surfaced by the orchestrator sibling's review: `ca03`'s PASS-2 verification note (on ca03) records verbatim — "a code/plan discrepancy independent of these findings: registry.py:279 lists T12 in `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` (deterministic), while the plan body lists T12 as LLM-routed." Corroborated from an independent pass.
  - Roster cross-check (this is a CONTRADICTION, not an absence — T12 IS owned/implemented): T12 the criterion ships (`plan_review_T12.md`, registered). The defect is that its ROUTING in b711's deliverable conflicts with the parent + descriptor; no sibling "fixes" the routing — `ca03` (orchestrator) merely DISPATCHES whatever `registry.overlay_triggers()` returns, so the deterministic T12 rule is live.
- scenarios:
  - CONSUMER IMPACT on sibling `7284` (eval suite): 7284 ships `T12-suppress-rebar` (a rebar library/CLI plan that MUST NOT fire T12, `expect: pass`, `mode: not-applicable-library-or-cli`) precisely to validate the parent's anti-FP. A rebar plan whose prose contains "deploy"/"rollback"/"rollout" (ubiquitous in rebar's own tickets — the parent's `claim`/signature/rollout language is full of it) will DETERMINISTICALLY trigger T12 via `registry.py:279`, then run the T12 overlay — re-creating the 7/19 false-fire the design corrected and the eval suppression case guards against. The deterministic trigger fires the overlay BEFORE the LLM router's not-applicable judgment can suppress it.
  - A project reading the parent's "T12 is LLM-routed for plans" and the b711 Scope expects no deterministic T12 keyword firing; the shipped `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` silently contradicts that expectation.
- impact: A settled parent design decision (T12 LLM-routed for plans, the core anti-FP for libraries/CLIs like rebar itself) is contradicted by this child's shipped artifact; the contradiction re-opens the exact false-fire mode the parent's round-4 experiment closed, and collides with sibling 7284's T12-suppression eval case. Calibration-INDEPENDENT (it is a routing-assignment defect, not threshold tuning), so not covered by the parent's "calibration is post-implementation" carve-out.
- checklist_item:
  - [ ] Reconcile T12's routing to the parent's FINALIZE CORRECTION + the canonical `make_crit_v8.py` LLM_ROUTED set: REMOVE the `"T12"` entry from `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` (registry.py:279) so T12 is LLM-routed for plans (matching b711's own Scope, the parent body, and `criteria_routing.json`'s `overlay_routing: "llm"`), and align b711's verify-note wording (drop "T12" from the stated deterministic low-FP set). Add/keep a test that a rebar-style plan containing "deploy"/"rollback" does NOT deterministically fire T12 (the 7284 `T12-suppress-rebar` case is the natural gate).
- suggested_fix: Delete the `"T12": r"\b(deploy|rollout|...|rollback|...)\b"` line from `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` (the deterministic low-FP set is then T5a/T5d/T7, matching the parent's "deterministic where low-FP — T1/T5a/T5d" framing); T12 routes via the LLM relevance router like T10/T11. Confirm `criteria_routing.json` T12 `overlay_routing` stays `"llm"` so the descriptor and the runtime trigger map agree.

═══ AFFIRMED-CLEAN modes (no finding) ═══
- SCOPE OVERLAP — CLEAN. b711 owns the T1-T12 overlay CATALOG + trigger routing (deterministic-vs-LLM); `ca03` owns the generic orchestrator that DISPATCHES triggered overlays; `012e` owns the DET floor P1-P8 (distinct from the Txx overlays). The parent disambiguates P7's destructive sniff (DET, 012e) from the T4 compat/destructiveness overlay (b711) — no double-ownership. b711's "What" listing G6 in the LLM relevance router is CONSISTENT with the canonical descriptor (`make_crit_v8.py:29` `LLM_ROUTED` includes G6) and with the parent's agent-tier set listing G6 — G6 is an LLM-routed AGENT-tier overlay-class criterion; b711 routes it, the criterion build is the agent-tier set's, no scope collision.
- CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS (routing posture, apart from T12) — CLEAN. b711's "deterministic-where-low-FP (T1/T5a/T5d) else LLM-routed (T8/T6/T9/G6/T10/T11/T12)" matches the parent's "deterministic where low-FP — T1/T5a/T5d; else LLM-routed — T8/T6/T5b/T5c/T9" and the make_crit_v8 LLM_ROUTED set. The only divergence is the shipped T12 deterministic rule (the Finding above); the stated DESIGN in b711's prose is consistent with the parent.
- DEPENDENCY GAP / ORDERING — CLEAN. b711 (overlay catalog + triggers) is consumed by `ca03`'s orchestrator (overlay routing) and feeds findings into Pass-1/2/3 (`1913`/`acc1`/`487d`); applies_at level gating lives in `ca03`'s `registry.applies()`, which b711's overlays declare into. No missing sequencing edge; overlays are correctly downstream of the registry/orchestrator spine.
- CONSUMER IMPACT (beyond T12) — CLEAN. The overlay catalog's outputs (findings carrying criteria[] = T-ids) are consumed field-agnostically by Pass-2/Pass-3 and recorded by `db7b`'s `_slim`; T2's FP6 anti-FP encoding is coordinated with `7284`'s calibration mandate. No drift.
- RESIDUAL REFERENCES (beyond T12) — CLEAN. b711's T1-T12 prompts all ship (`plan_review_T*.md`); P7→T4 "escalation" wording is a 012e-side narrative item (already noted on the 012e pairing), not a b711 dangling reference; b711 references no removed/renamed sibling artifact.

NET: ONE G4 finding — b711's shipped `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` makes T12 a DETERMINISTIC keyword trigger, contradicting the parent's settled FINALIZE CORRECTION (T12 LLM-routed for plans), b711's own Scope, and the canonical descriptor, and colliding with sibling 7284's T12-suppression eval case (independently corroborated by ca03's Pass-2 note). All other cross-child modes are consistent-by-design.
